title: Adirondack Red Wings

[Glens Falls Civic Center](/wiki/Glens_Falls_Civic_Center "Glens Falls Civic Center")
Games | Won | Lost | Tied | OTL | Points | Goals for | Goals against | Standing

were a minor professional [ice hockey](/wiki/Ice_hockey "Ice hockey") team in the [American Hockey League](/wiki/American_Hockey_League "American Hockey League"). They played in [Glens Falls, New York](/wiki/Glens_Falls,_New_York "Glens Falls, New York"), United States at the [Glens Falls Civic Center](/wiki/Glens_Falls_Civic_Center "Glens Falls Civic Center"). The team was affiliated with the [Detroit Red Wings](/wiki/Detroit_Red_Wings "Detroit Red Wings") of the [National Hockey League](/wiki/National_Hockey_League "National Hockey League").

The **Adirondack Red Wings** were a minor professional [ice hockey](/wiki/Ice_hockey "Ice hockey") team in the [American Hockey League](/wiki/American_Hockey_League "American Hockey League"). They played in [Glens Falls, New York](/wiki/Glens_Falls,_New_York "Glens Falls, New York"), United States at the [Glens Falls Civic Center](/wiki/Glens_Falls_Civic_Center "Glens Falls Civic Center"). The team was affiliated with the [Detroit Red Wings](/wiki/Detroit_Red_Wings "Detroit Red Wings") of the [National Hockey League](/wiki/National_Hockey_League "National Hockey League").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Adirondack_Red_Wings&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Desirous of promoting a winning atmosphere, Detroit ensured that the Adirondack Red Wings would have, for a minor league franchise, an unusually stable, veteran-laden roster. Veterans such as [Glenn Merkosky](/wiki/Glenn_Merkosky "Glenn Merkosky"), [Jody Gage](/wiki/Jody_Gage "Jody Gage"), [Greg Joly](/wiki/Greg_Joly "Greg Joly"), [Norm Maracle](/wiki/Norm_Maracle "Norm Maracle") and [Dennis Polonich](/wiki/Dennis_Polonich "Dennis Polonich") bolstered a team that saw over thirty players have 200 or more games with the franchise, including nine with over 300 and two (Merkosky and Joly) with over 400. In consequence, the Red Wings missed the playoffs only once in their twenty-year history. They played for the [Calder Cup](/wiki/Calder_Cup "Calder Cup") four times, winning each time.

The Red Wings' uniforms were identical to the parent club, with the white jersey featuring the distinctive red sleeves that the Detroit franchise has worn since 1956. During their final two seasons, the Adirondack Red Wings also wore a third jersey, based on Detroit's throwback design from [1991–92](/wiki/1991%E2%80%9392_NHL_season "1991–92 NHL season"), replacing the word "DETROIT" on the front of the jersey with the winged wheel logo.

The Red Wings prospered as Detroit's top affiliate. However, by the late 1990s, the parent club wanted to move the affiliation closer to Detroit in order to ease movement of players between the minors and the NHL. In early 1999, the Detroit Red Wings announced plans to move the team to [Rossford, Ohio](/wiki/Rossford,_Ohio "Rossford, Ohio")—a [Toledo](/wiki/Toledo,_Ohio "Toledo, Ohio") suburb—for the 2000–01 season. The Red Wings later suspended operations of the team following the 1998–99 season. As part of the agreement to relocate the franchise, the city of Rossford was to build a $48 million entertainment complex that included a 12,000-seat arena. However, the arena deal fell apart in late 2000.[[1]](#cite_note-1) The franchise remained dormant until 2002, when it was purchased by the ownership of the [National Basketball Association](/wiki/National_Basketball_Association "National Basketball Association")'s [San Antonio Spurs](/wiki/San_Antonio_Spurs "San Antonio Spurs") and resurrected as the [San Antonio Rampage](/wiki/San_Antonio_Rampage "San Antonio Rampage").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

**The franchise was replaced by:**

* [Adirondack IceHawks/Frostbite](/wiki/Adirondack_IceHawks "Adirondack IceHawks") of the [UHL](/wiki/United_Hockey_League "United Hockey League") (1999–2006).
* [Adirondack Phantoms](/wiki/Adirondack_Phantoms "Adirondack Phantoms") of the [AHL](/wiki/American_Hockey_League "American Hockey League") (2009–2014).
* [Adirondack Flames](/wiki/Adirondack_Flames "Adirondack Flames") of the [AHL](/wiki/American_Hockey_League "American Hockey League") (2014–2015).
* [Adirondack Thunder](/wiki/Adirondack_Thunder "Adirondack Thunder") of the [ECHL](/wiki/ECHL "ECHL") (2015–present).
